methods.description: Collection of diatom material and water samples from various habitats in southern Poland for chemical and diatom analysis.

steps.description: The field studies were conducted from 2008 to 2016 in various types of waters (mainly in flowing waters and on peat bogs) in southern Poland.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field studies in southern Poland from 2008 to 2016

steps.description: The material was collected from all available habitats: stones, mud or sand, and submerged plants.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from stones, mud, sand, and submerged plants

steps.description: Two types of samples (diatom material and water for chemical analyses) were collected from spring to autumn over the year (sites on rivers and streams) or only in spring and autumn (sites in peatland areas).
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom and water samples seasonally

methods.description: Measurement of pH and electrolytic conductivity at each site during field studies.

steps.description: The pH and electrolytic conductivity were measured in situ at every site, during the field studies.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH and conductivity in situ at each site

methods.description: Chemical analysis of water samples using ion chromatography in the laboratory.

steps.description: Other chemical parameters were measured using a Thermo scientific DIONEX ICS-5000 + DC ion chromatograph in the laboratory at the Department of Soil Science, Environmental Chemistry and Hydrology, University of Rzeszow.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chemical analysis using ion chromatography

methods.description: Identification and counting of diatom species using light microscopy.

steps.description: Diatoms were identified and counted at 1000× magnification under oil immersion using a Nikon ECLIPSE 80i and Carl Zeiss Axio Imager A2 light microscopes.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count diatoms using light microscopy

steps.description: The following literature was used for diatom identification: Krammer and Lange-Bertalot (Krammer & Lange-Bertalot, 1986), Lange-Bertalot (1996,1999) and Buczkó et al. (2009).
The species abundance was obtained by counting diatom valves up to 400 in random transects of the light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: count diatom valves to determine species abundance

steps.description: The species with over 5% share in a sample were considered as dominants.
steps.searchable_summary: identify dominant species with over 5% share
